# Mutation-testing equivalence file
#
# Documented-equivalent mutations to suppress from the survivor count.
# Each non-blank, non-comment line:
#
#     <file>:<line>:<op>:<original>:<mutated>    # optional comment
#
# Match is exact on all five fields, except line may be M-N for a range
# and op/original/mutated may each be `*` to match anything. When a
# mutant's fingerprint matches, it's reported as IGNORED rather than
# SURVIVED, and the effective kill rate is computed as
# killed / (total - ignored).
#
# Adding entries is a deliberate act:
#   - Verify the mutation is *truly* equivalent (no observable behaviour
#     change for any reasonable input the function sees).
#   - Cite the equivalence class in the trailing comment so the next
#     reviewer can re-verify.
#
# Equivalence classes (extend in docs/MUTATION-TESTING.md):
#   OPT  - early-return optimisation guard (correctness unaffected;
#          mutation only causes wasted work)
#   WGT  - composite-score weight constant where the literal ±1
#          perturbation can't shift bucketed output
#   LZT  - loop bound reading zero-initialised tail (e.g. unused
#          char_counts[] slots)
#
# Start empty. Earn each entry by triaging a real survivor.

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# src/complexity.c — Round 1 triage
# ------------------------------------------------------------
src/complexity.c:34:rel:<:<=          # LZT: char_counts[256] is read past array end; the >0 guard at L35 makes any value at that index unobservable (UB-tolerant on typical stacks)
src/complexity.c:52:const:1:0         # OPT: run_length init is overwritten on the first loop iteration's else branch (i=0 always differs from '\0')
src/complexity.c:167:const:10:11      # WGT: score cap unreachable — max composite is ~3.1 given the current 0.3/0.25/0.2/0.15/0.1 weights and component ranges
src/complexity.c:167:const:10:9       # WGT: same — even a 9.0 cap exceeds the max achievable composite
